description_long: This is a stylized wooden ship reminiscent of a Viking longship, featuring a single large square sail with alternating red and white vertical stripes on one side and plain white on the other. The hull is elongated and narrow, with a shallow draft and pointed bow and stern, both of which curve slightly upward. The deck is open and lined with a series of round shields mounted along the sides, and there are no visible cabins or enclosed superstructure. A single mast rises from the center of the ship, supported by rigging lines attached to the bow and stern. The ship's construction appears to be primarily wooden, with metal fastenings and fittings implied by the shield bosses and mast hardware. The overall design is simple yet functional, evoking historical seafaring vessels used for both transport and raiding.
description: A stylized wooden Viking-style longship with a single mast, striped sail, open deck, and round shields along the sides.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed bow, large striped sail, and shields lining the sides.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated hull, upward-curved bow and stern, and shield arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain white sail and a clear view of the open deck and stern curvature.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the hull profile, mast, and shield placement along the deck.
attribute_hull_design: The hull is a long, narrow displacement type with a shallow draft, pointed bow and stern, and a slight upward curve at both ends. There are no visible keels or bulbous bows, and the sides are lined with round shields.
attribute_superstructure_layout: There is no enclosed superstructure; the ship features a single open deck with a central mast and shield mounts along the gunwales. No cabins, bridges, or towers are present.
